linguisticChallenges.definiteSuffix: Romanian marks definiteness with a suffix (-ul/-le, -a, -lui) attached to the noun, similar to Bulgarian. MT must produce suffixed articles, not separate words.
linguisticChallenges.caseSystem: Romanian retains a case system (unique among Romance languages) with nominative-accusative and genitive-dative distinctions.
linguisticChallenges.diacritics: Romanian uses ș, ț, â, î, ă. Missing diacritics change meaning — 'tara' vs 'țara' (country). Old cedilla forms (ş, ţ) vs comma-below forms (ș, ț) create font/encoding issues.

dialects.classification: Standard Romanian based on Wallachian dialect. Moldovan is politically distinct but linguistically identical.

encyclopedic.history: Romanian is the only Romance language in Eastern Europe, surrounded by Slavic and Hungarian languages. It retained Latin grammar structure despite heavy Slavic vocabulary influence.
